title: 10 best herbal creams for enlarged spleen

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with enlarged spleen because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a bioactive compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are thought to contribute to the enlargement of the spleen. By applying topical turmeric cream, these compounds can directly target the affected area, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen. This natural approach may also help alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and tenderness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound, curcumin, helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the spleen, alleviating symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric cream help protect the spleen from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate conditions like an enlarged spleen.

Immune-modulating property: Turmeric's active compounds, particularly curcumin, have been shown to modulate immune function and reduce inflammation in the spleen, helping to alleviate symptoms of an enlarged spleen such as pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of enlarged spleen.
Barks: The barks of the turmeric plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce pain and swelling associated with an enlarged spleen, making it a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Grind 100g of dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the turmeric powder with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a clean glass jar, stirring well for 5 minutes.
Add 50g of beeswax pellets to the mixture, heating it in a double boiler until melted for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then whip with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy for 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass jars, sealing tightly and labeling as needed for use within 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest zingiber officinale roots, carefully selecting fresh and healthy specimens for maximum potency.
Cleanse the harvested zingiber officinale roots by rinsing them thoroughly with filtered water and gently scrubbing off any dirt.
Dry the cleaned zingiber officinale roots in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground zingiber officinale powder with distilled water in a 1:2 ratio, mixing thoroughly to create a smooth paste that can be stored in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with enlarged spleen because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections that can exacerbate the condition. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal garlic creams may provide relief and support for individuals suffering from an enlarged spleen.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh garlic bulbs extract their juice and filter it through cheesecloth to obtain a clear liquid.
Combine the filtered garlic juice with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool to around 120°f before adding 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of aloe vera gel.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and allow it to cool completely at room temperature for at least two hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Combine 250 grams of organic coconut oil with 50 grams of beeswax pellets in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the temperature reaches 180°f after 30 minutes.
Add 100 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin powder and 20 grams of vitamin e oil to the warm mixture, stirring well to combine.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for approximately one hour before whipping it with an electric mixer until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Transfer the finished cream to sterilized glass jars, store in the refrigerator at 40°f or below, and use within six months.

Silicic acid: Silicic acid, a component of milk thistle, has been found to improve blood vessel permeability and reduce edema (swelling), making it useful in reducing the size and discomfort associated with an enlarged spleen.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, which helps protect the liver and reduce inflammation associated with an enlarged spleen.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, they help soothe and calm inflammation in the spleen.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic silybum marianum dried flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, and vitamin e oil for the cream base preparation.
Mix 100g of dried silybum marianum flowers with 500ml of boiling water to create a decoction that will steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids, then combine the liquid with 200g of melted coconut oil and stir well.
Add 20g of beeswax and 5ml of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat it gently in a double boiler until the wax melts and the mixture thickens.
Pour the final cream into sterilized glass jars, seal them tightly, and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for enlarged spleen are topical ointments infused with natural extracts and essential oils, designed to provide relief from symptoms associated with an enlarged spleen. These creams help by reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and soothing discomfort. Examples of herbal creams that help include turmeric cream, ginger cream, and St. John's Wort cream, which have anti-inflammatory properties that ease pain and swelling. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved digestion, reduced fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being, ultimately enhancing their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat enlarged spleen with herbal creams is to apply topical applications that stimulate blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and enhance lymphatic function. Turmeric and ginger-based creams are effective in reducing swelling and pain, while aloe vera cream soothes the skin and promotes healing. Additionally, creams containing echinacea and goldenseal help boost immunity and combat infection. Apply these herbal creams topically 2-3 times a day to alleviate symptoms of an enlarged spleen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to enlarged spleen that are treated with herbal creams are various conditions affecting the lymphatic system, such as swollen lymph nodes and lymphedema. Herbal creams containing ingredients like calendula, tea tree oil, and arnica may help reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and promote healing. These creams can be used topically to soothe discomfort, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain associated with these conditions.
